explanation_box.text: This reptile's body is short and incapable of laying many eggs at once
This reptile's green color allows it to lay more eggs at once
The spotted pattern on the reptile's back prevented the reptile from laying many eggs at once
The green color of the reptile made it more fertile, but the spotted pattern on its back reduced its fertility
The reptile's lack of sharp claws made it somewhat fertile, but this fertility was reduced by its red color and the spotted pattern on its back
The green color of the reptile made it fertile, but its sharp claws and spotted back reduced its fertility
The reptile's green color and lack of claws makes it quite fertile, but its spotted back reduced its fertility
The reptile's red color and sharp claws reduces its fertility
The reptile's green color increases its fertility, but its sharp claws reduces its fertility to a greater extent
The reptiles red color and sharp claws greatly reduces its fertility
The reptile's green color and lack of sharp claws greatly increases its fertility
The reptile's lack of sharp claws increases its fertility, but its red color reduces its fertility by a greater amount
The red color of the reptile contributes significantly to its low fertility, while its lack of sharp claws seems to increase its fertility by a small amount
The combination of the reptile's red color and sharp claws greatly reduces its fertility
The reptile' green color increases its fertility somewhat, but its sharp claws reduces its fertility to a greater extent
The combination of the reptile's green color and sharp claws makes it very fertile

prompt_continuous: Reptiles varied in whether they were GREEN or RED. Did being GREEN or RED make a difference to fertility? If you did not notice this feature or believe it did not make a difference to fertility, please hit no and proceed.
question_text: Did color matter to the fertility of reptiles?

prompt_continuous: Reptiles varied in whether they had ROUND feet or POINTY, CURLY feet. Did having CURLY/POINTY or ROUND feet make a difference to fertility? If you did not notice this feature or believe it did not make a difference to fertility, please hit no and proceed.
question_text: Did foot shape matter to the fertility of reptiles?

feedback_text.text: I really enjoyed this experiment, as I believe it challenges my ability to analyze visual details and draw conclusions about those details in an engaging way. I made the mistake of focusing on the spotted pattern on the reptile's back as a potential factor that contributed to fertility, but realized about halfway through that all the reptiles seemed to have those spots. I believe that I was able to figure out the correct factors afterwards, which was pretty fun.
